summary: Phase 2 (P0/P1/P2) backend testing for ArtisanWeb. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_artisanweb_phase2.py — 19 tests covering: regenerate-logo, regenerate-hero (real Gemini, ~30s each), object storage proxy /api/files/{path} (200 + 404 + no-auth), /api/billing/me (free plan + 401), /api/billing/checkout (success + invalid package 400), /api/billing/status (200/xfail/404 for other user), free-tier 402 enforcement with French 'Pro' message, custom_domain/show_map/map_address persistence, lead public submission (RESEND_API_KEY empty silent-skip path), Pro bypass via DB pro_until grant, multi-tenant isolation on regenerate-logo/hero/billing-status. Result: 18 PASS + 1 XFAIL (documents the /api/billing/status 500 bug). Iteration 1 regression suite (28 tests) still passes.

critical.issue: P0 lead-email notification (Resend) is NOT WIRED. send_lead_notification_email is defined at server.py:516 but NEVER called from submit_lead (line 734-750). Therefore: (a) no notification email is ever sent to the artisan even when RESEND_API_KEY is set, and (b) the '[email skipped — no RESEND_API_KEY]' log line never appears (verified via grep on supervisor logs). Lead doc IS saved correctly, so DB persistence works, but the P0 feature is missing its trigger call.

Returns 500 Internal Server Error when polling a freshly-created checkout session. _apply_pro_credit_if_paid (server.py:835) calls stripe_checkout.get_checkout_status(session_id) which raises CheckoutError 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...' because the session_id returned by create_checkout_session under STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent is not retrievable from the real Stripe API. No try/except wraps this call — it propagates as an uncaught 500 to the client. Reproduction: POST /api/billing/checkout body={package_id:'pro_monthly', origin_url:<host>} -> get session_id -> immediately GET /api/billing/status/{session_id} -> 500. Mitigation: wrap get_checkout_status in try/except returning {payment_status:'unknown', applied:false, error:'stripe_lookup_failed'} OR upgrade STRIPE_API_KEY to a real test secret that supports retrieve.

minor.issue: When Gemini fails (e.g. quota), endpoint returns 502 'La génération a échoué' which is correct. Otherwise object-storage URL pattern is correct (/api/files/artisanweb/{logo|hero}/{user_id}/{uuid}.jpg).
Public, no auth required (intentional). 404 for unknown paths works. Content-Type is correctly preserved (image/jpeg observed). Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable — good.
metadata.days is stringified ('30'/'365') — fine for Stripe constraints, just noting that _apply_pro_credit_if_paid casts back via int(...).

action_items: WIRE send_lead_notification_email INTO submit_lead. After db.leads.insert_one(lead), use asyncio.create_task to fire-and-forget: public_url = f"{origin}/site/{slug}" (or use a config base url); owner_email = (await db.users.find_one({'id': site['user_id']}, {'email':1}))['email']. Without this, the entire P0 lead-email feature is dead code.

FIX /api/billing/status 500: wrap stripe_checkout.get_checkout_status in try/except CheckoutError in _apply_pro_credit_if_paid; on failure, return cached txn fields with payment_status='pending' or 'unknown' instead of raising. Also consider using sk_test_... real Stripe test key so retrieve actually works.
Optional: surface generation_method (claude|fallback) and image_generation_failed flags in /api/sites/generate response to help frontend detect AI degraded mode (carry-over from iteration 1).
Optional: Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md to mention pytest test_artisanweb_phase2.py invocation.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L516 send_lead_notification_email is defined but never invoked → P0 feature broken. Either remove the dead helper or hook it into submit_lead.

server.py L835 (_apply_pro_credit_if_paid) — no exception handling around external Stripe call. Always wrap third-party SDK calls in try/except and translate to 502/503 with structured detail; never let SDK exceptions bubble as 500.

server.py L607 free-tier check uses count_documents(user_id) — correct. But when delete_site is called, the count drops, so a Free user can keep recreating sites (acceptable for MVP, not enforced as 'first-site forever').
server.py L777 PACKAGES dict only allows pro_monthly/pro_yearly — good (server-side authoritative pricing, no client-amount injection).
server.py upload_image_bytes correctly tracks file metadata in db.files (storage_path, content_type, owner_id, kind, is_deleted). Good for future GDPR / soft delete.

server.py /api/files/{file_path:path} uses path:path FastAPI converter so slashes work (verified). Good. But there's no ownership check — any authenticated or unauthenticated client who knows a file path can fetch it. This is intentional for hero/logo to be embeddable in public sites; just be aware logos/hero contain no PII so it's acceptable.
server.py L922 startup hook does asyncio.to_thread(_init_storage_sync) — good. But subsequent _init_storage_sync calls reuse a module-level _storage_key; if the key expires there is no refresh logic.

test mode: STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent appears to be a placeholder that the emergentintegrations library treats specially for create_checkout_session but real Stripe rejects on retrieve. Document this clearly in /app/backend/.env or replace with a real Stripe test key for end-to-end status polling tests.
test_credentials: Dynamically generated TEST_ph2a_<uuid>@artisanweb.fr / testpass123 (per spec in /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: Suite creates 2 users + 2-3 sites + 1 payment_transactions row + 1 lead. TestZCleanup deletes all sites at end. Users + payment_transactions + db.files objects remain (intentional for audit trail). One pro_until is granted to user_a in DB to verify Pro bypass.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two regression suites exist now: /app/backend/tests/test_artisanweb.py (28 baseline tests) and /app/backend/tests/test_artisanweb_phase2.py (19 P0/P1/P2 tests). Combined run ~2.5 minutes due to 2× Gemini image generations. After the lead-email and billing/status fixes land: (1) flip XFAIL marker in TestBilling.test_billing_status_unpaid_for_initiated to plain assert, (2) add a test that posts a lead and asserts backend logs contain '[email skipped — no RESEND_API_KEY]' (or that the function was called via a mock). To grant Pro for testing without going through Stripe, directly set users.pro_until = ISO future timestamp in MongoDB (DB_NAME=test_database). PACKAGES days are stored as strings in metadata and cast to int in _apply_pro_credit_if_paid.
rca of the issue: Bug 1 (lead email not sent): root cause is missing call site — send_lead_notification_email is implemented but submit_lead never invokes it. Reproduction: (a) POST /api/auth/register; (b) POST /api/sites/generate; (c) POST /api/public/sites/{slug}/leads with valid body; (d) grep '/var/log/supervisor/backend.*.log' for 'email skipped' or 'Lead notification' → 0 hits. Fix: add `asyncio.create_task(send_lead_notification_email(owner_email, site['business_name'], lead, public_url))` after db.leads.insert_one in submit_lead, fetching owner email by site['user_id'] from db.users. Bug 2 (billing/status 500): Stripe SDK CheckoutError is unhandled. Reproduction: (a) POST /api/billing/checkout {package_id:'pro_monthly', origin_url:<host>}; (b) GET /api/billing/status/{session_id} immediately → 500 with stack trace 'No such checkout.session'. Mitigation: try/except CheckoutError in _apply_pro_credit_if_paid that returns cached txn fields with payment_status='pending'.
